input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## Step 1 — Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install the latest .NET SDK via winget
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows App SDK / WinUI3 requires the Windows SDK and build tools:

```powershell
# Install VS 2022 Build Tools with required components
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools

# Then add the required workloads via the VS Installer CLI:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\BuildTools" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktopBuildTools `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --includeRecommended --quiet --wait

# OR if using full VS Community/Professional:
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community
# (select ".NET Multi-platform App UI development" workload in installer)
```

## Step 4 — Install Android SDK & Accept Licenses

The MAUI workload bundles Android SDK management, but you may need JDK + SDK:

```powershell
# Install OpenJDK (required by Android tooling)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Verify Java
java --version

# Discover where MAUI installed the Android SDK
dotnet android sdk info

# If Android SDK is missing, set it up manually:
# Default location after workload install:
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
# Or check:
$androidSdk = (dotnet android sdk info | Select-String "SDK Path").ToString().Split(":")[1].Trim()

# Accept all Android SDK licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# List installed Android platforms & pick what you need
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "platforms;"

# Install a specific API level (e.g., 35 for latest)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0"
```

## Step 5 — Set Up Android Emulator (optional)

```powershell
# Install system image + emulator
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" "emulator"

# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Enable Windows Hypervisor (run elevated)
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName "HypervisorPlatform" -All -NoRestart
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName "VirtualMachinePlatform" -All -NoRestart
# Reboot after this
```

## Step 6 — Enable Developer Mode (Windows target)

```powershell
# Required for sideloading Windows apps
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## Step 7 — Create & Build a Test Project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n TestMauiApp
cd TestMauiApp

# Build for Windows (WinUI3)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## Step 8 — Dynamic Version Discovery Cheatsheet

```powershell
# Find latest .NET SDK available
winget show Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9 --versions

# Find installed target frameworks dynamically
dotnet --list-sdks

# Find which Android API your MAUI version targets by default
grep -i "android" TestMauiApp/*.csproj
# Or in PowerShell:
Select-String -Path *.csproj -Pattern "TargetFrameworks"

# Find available Windows SDK versions on your machine
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include" | Select Name

# Check MAUI workload version
dotnet workload list
```

---

**Key gotcha:** If `dotnet build -f net9.0-windows*` fails with missing Windows SDK errors, install the Windows 10/11 SDK via `winget install Microsoft.WindowsSDK.10.0.19041` (adjust version to match your TFM).
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
